---
title: "causalCalibration"
---

# Overview

`causalCalibration` is a workflow-oriented package for calibrating heterogeneous treatment effect predictions after you have already trained a treatment-effect model.

It is for applied researchers who:

- already have treatment-effect predictions from some learner,
- want calibrated effect scores they can interpret more directly,
- want to avoid spending a separate holdout split on calibration when cross-fitted predictions are available,
- want diagnostics that quantify how much miscalibration remains.
- want guidance about overlap, target population, and whether `loss="dr"` or `loss="r"` is the better fit.

## Main workflows

### Standard calibration

Use `fit_calibrator()` when you have one prediction per observation and want a single calibration map.

- a vector of effect predictions,
- treatment and outcome data,
- nuisance estimates for the chosen loss.

### Cross-calibration

Use `fit_cross_calibrator()` when your underlying HTE model was trained with cross-fitting and you want to fit and calibrate in sample without carving out a separate calibration set.

Cross-calibration uses:

- pooled out-of-fold predictions to fit the calibration map,
- an `n x K` matrix of fold-specific predictions to produce calibrated predictions.

## Documentation map

- [Getting Started](getting-started.qmd): installation, mental model, and the package workflow at a glance
- [Standard Calibration](standard-calibration.qmd): when to use ordinary calibration and how to fit it
- [Cross-Calibration](cross-calibration.qmd): the central in-sample workflow for cross-fitted predictors
- [Diagnostics](diagnostics.qmd): how to quantify remaining miscalibration, report original-population vs overlap-targeted error, and interpret the outputs
- [Losses and Methods](choosing-losses-and-methods.qmd): how to choose `dr` vs `r` and which calibrator to use
- [API Reference](reference.qmd): function-by-function contracts, argument requirements, and common mistakes

## Source workflows

- Python notebook: [examples/python-workflow.ipynb](https://github.com/Larsvanderlaan/causalCalibration/blob/main/examples/python-workflow.ipynb)
- R vignette: [r/causalCalibration/vignettes/getting-started.Rmd](https://github.com/Larsvanderlaan/causalCalibration/blob/main/r/causalCalibration/vignettes/getting-started.Rmd)

## Method references

- Causal calibration and cross-calibration: [van der Laan et al. (2023)](https://proceedings.mlr.press/v202/van-der-laan23a.html)
- R-learner: [Nie and Wager (2021)](https://academic.oup.com/biomet/article/108/2/299/5911092)
- DR-learner theory: [Kennedy (2020)](https://doi.org/10.48550/arXiv.2004.14497)
- Calibration error estimation: [Xu and Yadlowsky (2022)](https://arxiv.org/abs/2203.13364)
